tagline: General-purpose AI function for querying models.
what_it_does: `ai_query` is a versatile AI function that allows users to query any supported AI model directly from SQL or Python. It provides full control over the model, prompt, and parameters, distinguishing it from task-specific AI functions. This function is designed for use with Databricks-hosted models, provisioned throughput models, and custom or external models, enabling flexible querying for various use cases.
why_it_matters: The availability of `ai_query` empowers data teams to leverage AI models more effectively, enhancing productivity and flexibility in data processing tasks. By allowing precise control over model parameters and prompts, organizations can optimize their AI workflows for better performance and tailored outputs, ultimately driving more insightful data-driven decisions.
plain_summary: ai query is a versatile AI function that allows users to query any supported AI model directly from SQL or Python. It provides full control over the model, prompt, and parameters, distinguishing it from task specific AI functions.

capabilities.title: Flexible Model Querying
capabilities.desc: Query any supported AI model with customizable prompts and parameters.

capabilities.title: Batch Inference Support
capabilities.desc: Optimized for high-throughput batch workloads, enhancing processing efficiency.

capabilities.title: Integration with Unity AI Gateway
capabilities.desc: Automatically routes requests through Unity AI Gateway for usage tracking.

capabilities.title: Support for Custom Models
capabilities.desc: Allows querying of custom and external models for tailored AI solutions.

prerequisites: Databricks Runtime 18.2 or above is required.
Serverless compute is required; not supported on classic compute clusters.
Not available on Pro or Classic SQL warehouses.
use_cases: Control over model parameters for specific outputs.
High-throughput batch processing for large datasets.
Automated data extraction from unstructured data.
limitations: Not supported on classic compute clusters.

steps.title: Enable Serverless Compute
steps.desc: Ensure your Databricks workspace is configured for serverless compute.

steps.title: Check Runtime Version
steps.desc: Verify that your Databricks Runtime is version 18.2 or above.

steps.title: Select Supported Models
steps.desc: Choose from Databricks-hosted or provisioned throughput models for optimal performance.

steps.title: Use SQL or Python
steps.desc: Implement `ai_query` in your SQL queries or Python scripts as needed.

steps.title: Monitor Usage
steps.desc: Utilize Unity AI Gateway for tracking and optimizing your AI queries.

key_takeaway: `ai_query` is now generally available, enabling flexible and powerful querying of AI models directly from SQL or Python, enhancing data processing capabilities.
